Osnabrück Vision AI (OSVIA) Lab

The OSVIA Lab researches methods that teach machines to see – from the basics of classical computer vision to modern deep learning approaches. Our goal is to develop algorithms that process visual data reliably, efficiently, and explainably. We work on problems ranging from the recognition and analysis of complex scenes to 3D reconstruction and robust machine learning. With our research, we bridge the gap between theory and application – for advances in science, industry, and society.

Universität Osnabrück: AG Computer Vision and Robust AI

Bergische Universität Wuppertal: AG Applied and Computational Mathematics

Projects

Ongoing and recent work with our research and industry partners.

  • Unified Uncertainty Estimation for Fine-tuned Open-Vocabulary Models in Image Classification and Object Detection

    Making modern AI systems more reliable and transparent when operating in open-world environments.

    DFG UOS

    2026–2028

  • Project REFRAME

    Investigate the possibilities and limitations of so-called foundation models in computer-aided image recognition and to develop new methods for improving these models.

    BMFTR UOS

    2024–2027

  • Project RELiABEL

    How to make the interpretation of image data for automated driving more reliable.

    BMFTR UOS

    2024–2027

  • Just Scan It 3D

    Easy creation of 3D models with photogrammetry and AI

    EFRE BUW

    2024–2027

  • Cooperation with QUT Center for Robotics

    Exploring the frontiers of foundation models in computer vision.

    DAAD QUT BUW

    2024–2025

  • BMBF Junior Research Group UnrEAL

    Making the use of artificial intelligence safer.

    BMFTR BUW

    2022–2025 (finished)

Team

A multidisciplinary group committed to understanding and improving AI robustness in real-world scenarios.

Meet the Team

News

More News

Best Student Paper Award at VISAPP 2026

Apr 17, 2026 Paper VISAPP 2026

Our doctoral student Martha Teiko T. received the Best Student Paper Award at VISAPP 2026 for the paper “FutrTrack A Camera-LiDAR Fusion Transformer for 3D Multiple Object Tracking,” co-authored by Ori Maoz.

Read more Project page

Project LiDiMoVer

Mar 23, 2026 Project BUW EU NRW

LiDiMoVer is an innovative smart city research initiative focused on advancing urban mobility.

Read more

Paper accepted at CVPR 2026

Mar 10, 2026 Paper CVPR 2026

Our paper PRIMU Uncertainty Estimation for Novel Views in Gaussian Splatting from Primitive-Based Representations of Error and Coverage” has been accepted for publication at CVPR 2026.

Read more Project page

Inaugural lecture by Prof. Rottmann and Prof. Mossakowski

Jan 20, 2026 UOS

On 20.01.2026, the Institute of Computer Science celebrated "50 years of computer science at Osnabrück University". Prior to this, Prof. Rottmann and Prof. Mossakowski presented their research areas in their inaugural lectures.

Read more

Paper accepted at WACV 2026

Dec 20, 2025 Paper WACV 2026

Our paper “Can We Challenge Open-Vocabulary Object Detectors with Generated Content in Street Scenes?” has been accepted to WACV 2026.

Read more Paper

Workshop on Uncertainty Quantification at ControlExpert

Dec 12, 2025 Workshop

The OSVIA lab joined a scientific workshop hosted at ControlExpert together with the research team of Prof. Kira Maag from Heinrich Heine University Düsseldorf and colleagues from ControlExpert.
